raw_consumer_statement:
On XXXX, I Paid {$100.00} For A Warranty Repair, When The Technician Arrived He Explained The Defect Wasn't Covered And I Should Contact A Plumber. When The Plumber Arrived Next Day He Saw That The Issue Appeared In Two Parts. A Part Not Covered But Also A Crack In The Part Of The Fridge That Was Covered. Since I'm Already Paying For The Plumbers Visit, He Was Able To Remedy Both Parts. After The Job Was Completed I Called Back Sears And Explained What Occurred And Requested A Refund Of The Deductible, They Were Unwilling Because A Technician Had Already Been Sent Out. They Took No Responsibility For The Misdiagnosis. They Offered To Send The Tech Back Out, But At This Point The Issue Had Already Been Resolved. I Need To Be Made Whole For This {$100.00} Charge. 

In the rebuttal I received from Sears via the Merchant they are stating that The service provider provided a diagnosis, and the customer chose to have a plumber complete the job. That claim is incorrect. I had no option but to hire a plumber since the technician stated it wasn't covered and I needed to hire a plumber. Only when the plumber that I hired came out the next day explained that Sears should have fixed the issue since the defect also included a crack in the water supply line that a part of the fridge, I even included photos with my original dispute.
